headline: Ford Q1 Sales Slide Raises Questions On EV Plans And Valuation
summary: Ford Motor (NYSE:F) reported a nearly 9% decline in Q1 U.S. sales, linked to affordability pressures and inventory issues. A fire at a key aluminum supplier affected vehicle availability, contributing to production constraints during the quarter. Electric vehicle sales fell, while sales of larger SUVs rose and Ford's U.S. market share edged higher. Ford Motor sits at the center of the U.S. auto market, with a mix that spans trucks, SUVs, and electric vehicles. The latest quarter underscores...

headline: Can Ford Justify Record CEO Compensation After Record Recalls?
summary: Management knows it has a quality problem and has been tackling it. But progress hasn't been as transparent or obvious as investors would hope.

headline: How The Ford Motor (F) Investment Story Is Shifting With EV Resets And Margin Debates
summary: Ford Motor’s updated fair value estimate edges from US$13.76 to US$14.09, a small shift that still matters if you are tracking how analysts frame upside versus risk. Recent research shows that some firms are raising targets into the mid teens, while others trim or hold around US$12 to US$13, reflecting very different views on Ford’s execution on EVs and its high margin truck and SUV business. Read on to see what is driving these moves and how you can keep up with the evolving Ford...
